title: The Atom-Smashing Scientist
story: Ernest Lawrence, a brilliant scientist, was born in Canton, South Dakota on August 8, 1901. He grew up to invent the cyclotron, a powerful machine that speeds up tiny particles to study atoms. title: Tycho Brahe's Star-Gazing Castle

title: Brooklyn's Unexpected Twister
story: Whirling winds shocked New Yorkers in 2007 when a powerful tornado touched down on August 8. activity_question: What did India agree to give Bhutan in 1946?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for countries to be generous with their neighbors?

title: Secret War Meeting in Quebec
story: Shhh! Top military leaders gathered in Quebec for a secret meeting on August 8, 1943. These 'Chiefs of Staff' from Allied countries planned how to defeat Germany in World War II. They discussed big ideas like invading France and using new weapons. This important meeting helped the Allies work together to win the war.
activity_question: Where did the Chiefs of Staff meet in 1943?
follow_up_question: Why do you think they kept the meeting secret?

title: A Dutch Poet's Rhymes for Little Minds
story: Hieronymus van Alphen, a Dutch poet famous for his children's poems, was born in Gouda, The Netherlands in 1746. On August 8 of that year, the world welcomed a writer who would create delightful rhymes for young readers. Van Alphen's collection 'Proeve van kleine gedigen voor Kinder' brought joy to many little ones with its playful verses.
activity_question: Where was Hieronymus van Alphen born?
follow_up_question: What kind of poems did Hieronymus van Alphen write?

title: Wright Flyer Gets a Wheel Upgrade
story: Zoom! The Wright brothers' famous airplane got a cool new feature in 1910. On August 8, the US Army added three wheels to their Wright Flyer. Before this, planes landed on skids - like sleds! The new tricycle landing gear made takeoffs and landings smoother and safer. It was a big step forward in airplane design that we still use today.
activity_question: What did the US Army add to the Wright Flyer?
follow_up_question: Why do you think wheels are better for landing than skids?
